description: New infrastructure projects extracted from Twitter data and verified via web research

budget.notes: Built on 2.86 acres within 61.59 acre campus on NH-544

timeline.completionNotes: Inaugurated November 5, 2024 by CM MK Stalin

media.photoCaption: Six-storey IT facility with startup spaces

sources.title: The South First - ELCOT IT Park Inauguration
News Today - Stalin inaugurates Elcot facility
Construction World - Vilankurichi IT Park

projects.notes: Six-storey facility; 2.94 lakh sq ft total built-up area; 1.56 lakh sq ft dedicated IT space; Designated startup areas; Expected to provide employment for 3,500+ people; Seven IT companies received allotment orders; Plans for two more buildings on remaining 17.17 acres for 35,000 jobs

budget.notes: Modern exhibition facility with technological amenities

sources.title: Dinakaran - CM Stalin inspection
Exhibition Globe - Island Grounds development
Live Chennai - CMDA Exhibition Centre

projects.notes: 1.63 lakh sq ft (163,039 sq ft) built-up area; Ground + 2 storeys rising to 24 metres; 3.10 acres (scaled from original 18 acres due to CRZ-II constraints); 20 modular exhibition stalls; Amphitheatre; Waterfront promenade; Public plaza; Landscaped gardens; Parking; Restaurant; Children's play area; Part of 135-acre Island Grounds redevelopment

timeline.completionNotes: Perumbakkam depot by August 2025; Full deployment by February 2026

sources.title: Sustainable Bus - Chennai 120 electric buses
ITDP - Chennai E-Bus Transition
Native Planet - 625 Electric Buses

projects.notes: Charging and maintenance infrastructure at 6 depots: Perambur-II, Tondiarpet-I, Vyasarpadi, Poonamallee, KK Nagar-I, Perumbakkam-I; Part of Rs 959 crore e-bus scheme with World Bank funding (70%); First phase: 625 low-floor electric buses at Rs 697 crore total; 225 air-conditioned + 400 non-AC buses

budget.notes: Phase 1A: Rs 62.42 crore + Phase 1B: Rs 50.68 crore

timeline.completionNotes: PWD invited bids for Phase 1 construction

sources.title: India Investment Grid - Mannargudi Ring Road Project
NBMCW - PWD Bids for Mannargudi Ring Road
News On Projects - Mannargudi 22 km ring road

projects.notes: Phase 1A: km 0/0 to 5/610 connecting SH63 (Thanjavur-Mannargudi-Kodikkarai Road) with MD840; Phase 1B: km 5/610 to 11/900 connecting MD840 back to SH63; Total Phase 1: 11.9 km; Full ring road planned for 22 km connecting 3 State Highways and rural roads; Villages: Moondramsethi, Bhamini, Aravathur, Ramapuram, Kailasanathar Koil, Nangamsethi, Serankulam, Koppiralayam

name.en: Dr. M.S. Swaminathan Wetland Eco Park
name.ta: டாக்டர் எம்.எஸ். சுவாமிநாதன் ஈரநில சுற்றுச்சூழல் பூங்கா

timeline.completionNotes: Inaugurated March 7, 2025 by CM MK Stalin

media.photoCaption: First large eco-wetland park in Chennai

sources.title: Citizen Matters - Porur Sponge Park
ICLEI CityTalk - Chennai wetland sponge park
Podhuva.com - Swaminathan Wetland Park opening

projects.notes: 16.63 acres on Sri Ramachandra University's Open Space Reservation land; Chennai's first wetland sponge park; Developed by CMDA with Sponge Collaborative; 600-metre boardwalk; Pollinator garden; Basketball court; Multi-use sports area; Children's play space; Elevated boardwalks; Seating areas; Landscaped channels; Named after agricultural scientist Dr. M.S. Swaminathan

budget.notes: Asia's largest ornamental fish market complex

media.photoCaption: Modern fish market near Villivakkam-Padi flyover

sources.title: News Karnataka - Kolathur Fish Market Overhaul
Live Chennai - Modern Fish Market Complex
Fishery News - Chennai Ornamental Fish Centre

projects.notes: Asia's largest colorful fish stall; India's second largest ornamental fish market; Over 1,000 fish species including Oscars, Gourami, sucker fish, guppies, carp, goldfish; Current monthly revenue Rs 300 crore; Developed by CMDA; Part of North Chennai Development Plan; Modern infrastructure for fish trade

budget.notes: Initial Rs 100 crore expanded to Rs 425 crore

timeline.completionNotes: Restoration work launched July 2025; 68 temples completed by October 2025

sources.title: The Print Hindi - Stalin launches 63 temple restoration
Mangalorean - Rs 425 crore temple restoration
Social News XYZ - Temple restoration plan

projects.notes: Restoration of temples over 1,000 years old; Initial phase: 63 temples at Rs 100 crore (July 2025); Expanded to Rs 425 crore (October 2025); 68 temples completed using government, temple, and donor funds; Gold Monetisation Scheme: 1,074.123 kg purified gold from 21 temples deposited in SBI earning Rs 17.76 crore annual interest for temple development
